title: NOC 14200 – Accounting and Related Clerks | GTR Immigration
description: Accounting and Related Clerks play a crucial role in maintaining financial accuracy, budgeting processes, and transaction records across public and private organizations in Canada. Classified under NOC 14200, this occupation has experienced strong post-pandemic hiring growth and is expected to remain in steady demand—creating realistic immigration opportunities through employer-supported work permits, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.teer: Increased regulatory reporting and financial complianceBusiness expansion and bookkeeping needsEntry-level TEER occupation with career mobilityEmployer-supported recruitmentGrowth in payroll and accounts management
classification.major_group: 142 – Financial, insurance, and related administrative support workers
classification.minor_group: 1420 – Financial, insurance, and related administrative support workers

duties: Accounting and Related Clerks may perform some or all of the following:
Calculate, prepare, and issue financial documents such as invoices, inventory reports, and account statements
Code, batch, enter, verify, and reconcile transactions including payroll, purchase orders, cheques, and bank statements
Compile budget data based on estimated revenues, expenses, and previous budgets
Prepare cost statements and financial reports
Calculate material, overhead, and operating costs
Respond to customer inquiries and resolve billing issues
Perform clerical duties such as filing, word processing, photocopying, and record maintenance
employment_requirements: Completion of college accounting or bookkeeping courses (sometimes required)
Certification from recognized Canadian accounting bodies may be an asset
Some roles require employees to be bondable
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to generate approximately 57,600 new positions for Accounting and Related Clerks, while about 51,000 job seekers are expected to enter the labour market—making provincial nomination strategies, employer sponsorship, and targeted immigration planning especially valuable for overseas applicants.
additional_information: Accounting and Related Clerks are in demand across both public and private sectors nationwide. Hiring has expanded rapidly since the pandemic and is expected to continue over the coming years.
Accounting firms and audit officesGovernment departments and agenciesLogistics and shipping companiesFinancial institutionsCorporate finance departmentsRetail and wholesale operations
Annual salaries typically range between $32,300 and $64,900 CAD, depending on province, experience, and specialization.
